fields.description:
## Why

`src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` (~line 112) validates titles with `if not title:`,
which is falsy only for the empty string — a whitespace-only title such as `"   "` is
truthy and is accepted. Per the decision for this epic, whitespace-only titles must be
rejected. Separately, cross-facade parity tests use only simple ASCII values, and the
create path's empty/`>255` title rejection has code enforcement but only an edit-path
test, leaving input-handling contracts thinly covered.

## What

Committed policy decisions (so the test oracle is unambiguous):
- **Whitespace validation:** reject a title iff `title.strip()` is empty (covers `""`,
  spaces, tabs, newlines). A title with surrounding whitespace but real content (e.g.
  `"  hi  "`) is ACCEPTED and **stored as-is** — validation must not silently mutate the
  value, so round-trip assertions expect the original bytes back.
- **Unicode normalization:** **preserve as-is (byte-identical round-trip); do NOT
  normalize** — with ONE documented pre-existing exception: `create_core`/`edit_core`
  already substitute `U+2192 (→)` → `->` (`src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:121` and
  `:385`). That substitution is OUT OF SCOPE for this story and is retained; U+2192 is
  therefore EXCLUDED from the byte-identity corpus (or included only as an explicit
  "documented normalization" case asserting `→` becomes `->`). Composed (NFC) and
  decomposed (NFD) forms of the same string are otherwise treated as distinct values,
  each round-tripping unchanged — the oracle is per-form byte-identity, not cross-form
  equality.
- **Rejection identity (across facades):** "rejected identically" does NOT mean an
  identical wire representation — it means all three facades reject with the SAME
  human-readable message substring on their facade-appropriate surface: CLI → nonzero
  exit + message on stderr; library → `CommandError` (or the documented exception type)
  carrying the message; MCP → an error result carrying the message. The shared invariant
  is the message + "all three reject," not a shared exit code.

Then:
- Reject whitespace-only titles consistently across the CLI, library, and MCP
  create/edit surfaces.
- Add a create-path test for empty and `>255`-char title rejection.
- Add a curated cross-facade text corpus for values representable through all three
  facades; handle argv-impossible values (NUL) in library/MCP tests only.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` (title validation)
- `tests/interfaces/facades/` (corpus + create-path validation tests)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A whitespace-only title (spaces/tabs/newlines) is rejected on create AND edit
      across CLI, library, and MCP, with a clear error.
- [ ] Create-path tests assert rejection of an empty title and a `>255`-char title.
- [ ] A title with surrounding whitespace + content (`"  hi  "`) is accepted and stored
      byte-identically (no silent trimming).
- [ ] A shared corpus (leading `-`/`--`, quotes, backslashes, LF/CRLF, emoji/non-BMP,
      NFC and NFD forms of one string as SEPARATE byte-identity cases, and a string of
      ≥1000 characters) round-trips byte-identically — EXCEPT U+2192, handled per the
      documented `→`→`->` exception — or is rejected identically (per the rejection-
      identity definition above) across all three facades; persisted/replayed content and
      structured output are asserted, with no stdout/stderr contamination.
- [ ] NUL / other argv-impossible values are covered via library/MCP tests only.
- [ ] Breaking-change handling: this is a user-visible tightening (callers passing
      whitespace previously succeeded). Document a back-out/expand-contract note, and
      confirm whether any stored tickets have whitespace-only titles (a one-off store
      scan) so no migration is silently required.
- [ ] User-facing docs updated: CLI help text / API reference and a CHANGELOG entry
      note the new whitespace-title rejection.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
